revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]]" to "<nowiki>[[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]</nowiki>"

revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} Use {{tl|ISSN link}} to produce a link or pair of links to the [[WorldCat]] search page for an [[ISSN]]. Unlike {{tl|ISSN}}, this template does not prefix the link with "[[ISSN]] "; and if multiple ISSNs are provided they will be tagged as being for "(print)" and "(web)". ==Usage== In any context where you need to show an ISSN linked to a [[WorldCat]] search, but where the inclusion of the "[[ISSN]] " prefix would be distracting, or where you need to provide both the ISSN (print) and eISSN (web/online/digital) for a publication, you can use {{tl|ISSN link}} to generate the needed markup. The template was created for use with [[List of Cambridge University Press journals]] (several hundred ISSNs in a table, with a separate column for the ISSNs). If you only need a single or a few ISSNs, you'll be better off just using {{tl|ISSN}}. The template takes two positional parameters, and at least one is required. If both are given, the first is assumed to be the ISSN for the print edition and the second to be the online edition, and both will be tagged accordingly. If only one parameter is given, no assumption is made regarding print vs. online and no tagging is done. * |1=: If |2= is also provided, this is the ISSN for the print edition, and will be tagged with "(print)". If this is the only parameter passed in, no assumption will be made as to its type and no tag will be provided. * |2=: The ISSN of the online edition. This parameter is optional, but if given will produce a linked ISSN tagged with "(web)". ==Examples== ===One argument=== {{ISSN link|1234-5678}} → {{ISSN link|1234-5678}} ===Two arguments=== {{ISSN link|1234-5678|9876-5432}} → {{ISSN link|1234-5678|9876-5432}} ===Two arguments without a line break=== {{ISSN link|1234-5678|9876-5432|br=;}} → {{ISSN link|1234-5678|9876-5432|br=;}} ==Notes and error detection== ===Finding the ISSN of a publication=== * Find the ISSN by [http://grlinker.coalliance.org/grbin/ilu_rmt.cgi searching with the publication's name] on the [[Colorado Alliance of Research Libraries]] web site. ===Note=== An ISSN is always eight digits long. Scroll down the WorldCat page to find the ISSN. Do not rely on the digits shown in the WorldCat URL for the particular publication, since the URL sometimes shows only seven digits, which is invalid to use as the ISSN. ===Errors=== Articles with invalid ISSNs (as determined by the template) will be placed in {{cl|Articles with invalid ISSNs}}. ==See also== * [[Template:Infobox newspaper]] * [[Template:Infobox journal]] * [[Template:ISSN]] * [[Template:OCLC]] * [[Template:OCLC search link]] * [[Template:UPC]] {{basepage subpage| [[Category:External link templates]] [[Category:Catalog lookup templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} Use {{tl|ISSN link}} to produce a link or pair of links to the [[WorldCat]] search page for an [[:en:ISSN|ISSN]]. Unlike {{tl|ISSN}}, this template does not prefix the link with "[[:en:ISSN|ISSN]] "; and if multiple ISSNs are provided they will be tagged as being for "(print)" and "(web)". ==Usage== In any context where you need to show an ISSN linked to a [[WorldCat]] search, but where the inclusion of the "[[:en:ISSN|ISSN]] " prefix would be distracting, or where you need to provide both the ISSN (print) and eISSN (web/online/digital) for a publication, you can use {{tl|ISSN link}} to generate the needed markup. The template was created for use with [[List of Cambridge University Press journals]] (several hundred ISSNs in a table, with a separate column for the ISSNs). If you only need a single or a few ISSNs, you'll be better off just using {{tl|ISSN}}. The template takes two positional parameters, and at least one is required. If both are given, the first is assumed to be the ISSN for the print edition and the second to be the online edition, and both will be tagged accordingly. If only one parameter is given, no assumption is made regarding print vs. online and no tagging is done. * |1=: If |2= is also provided, this is the ISSN for the print edition, and will be tagged with "(print)". If this is the only parameter passed in, no assumption will be made as to its type and no tag will be provided. * |2=: The ISSN of the online edition. This parameter is optional, but if given will produce a linked ISSN tagged with "(web)". ==Examples== ===One argument=== {{ISSN link|1234-5678}} → {{ISSN link|1234-5678}} ===Two arguments=== {{ISSN link|1234-5678|9876-5432}} → {{ISSN link|1234-5678|9876-5432}} ===Two arguments without a line break=== {{ISSN link|1234-5678|9876-5432|br=;}} → {{ISSN link|1234-5678|9876-5432|br=;}} ==Notes and error detection== ===Finding the ISSN of a publication=== * Find the ISSN by [http://grlinker.coalliance.org/grbin/ilu_rmt.cgi searching with the publication's name] on the [[Colorado Alliance of Research Libraries]] web site. ===Note=== An ISSN is always eight digits long. Scroll down the WorldCat page to find the ISSN. Do not rely on the digits shown in the WorldCat URL for the particular publication, since the URL sometimes shows only seven digits, which is invalid to use as the ISSN. ===Errors=== Articles with invalid ISSNs (as determined by the template) will be placed in {{cl|Articles with invalid ISSNs}}. ==See also== * [[Template:Infobox newspaper]] * [[Template:Infobox journal]] * [[Template:ISSN]] * [[Template:OCLC]] * [[Template:OCLC search link]] * [[Template:UPC]] {{basepage subpage| [[Category:External link templates]] [[Category:Catalog lookup templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local/ILL link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} Use {{tl|ISSN link}} to produce a link or pair of links to the [[WorldCat]] search page for an [[:en:ISSN|ISSN]]. Unlike {{tl|ISSN}}, this template does not prefix the link with "[[:en:ISSN|ISSN]] "; and if multiple ISSNs are provided they will be tagged as being for "(print)" and "(web)". ==Usage== In any context where you need to show an ISSN linked to a [[WorldCat]] search, but where the inclusion of the "[[:en:ISSN|ISSN]] " prefix would be distracting, or where you need to provide both the ISSN (print) and eISSN (web/online/digital) for a publication, you can use {{tl|ISSN link}} to generate the needed markup. The template was created for use with [[List of Cambridge University Press journals]] (several hundred ISSNs in a table, with a separate column for the ISSNs). If you only need a single or a few ISSNs, you'll be better off just using {{tl|ISSN}}. The template takes two positional parameters, and at least one is required. If both are given, the first is assumed to be the ISSN for the print edition and the second to be the online edition, and both will be tagged accordingly. If only one parameter is given, no assumption is made regarding print vs. online and no tagging is done. * |1=: If |2= is also provided, this is the ISSN for the print edition, and will be tagged with "(print)". If this is the only parameter passed in, no assumption will be made as to its type and no tag will be provided. * |2=: The ISSN of the online edition. This parameter is optional, but if given will produce a linked ISSN tagged with "(web)". ==Examples== ===One argument=== {{ISSN link|1234-5678}} → {{ISSN link|1234-5678}} ===Two arguments=== {{ISSN link|1234-5678|9876-5432}} → {{ISSN link|1234-5678|9876-5432}} ===Two arguments without a line break=== {{ISSN link|1234-5678|9876-5432|br=;}} → {{ISSN link|1234-5678|9876-5432|br=;}} ==Notes and error detection== ===Finding the ISSN of a publication=== * Find the ISSN by [http://grlinker.coalliance.org/grbin/ilu_rmt.cgi searching with the publication's name] on the [[Colorado Alliance of Research Libraries]] web site. ===Note=== An ISSN is always eight digits long. Scroll down the WorldCat page to find the ISSN. Do not rely on the digits shown in the WorldCat URL for the particular publication, since the URL sometimes shows only seven digits, which is invalid to use as the ISSN. ===Errors=== Articles with invalid ISSNs (as determined by the template) will be placed in {{cl|Articles with invalid ISSNs}}. ==See also== * [[Template:Infobox newspaper]] * [[:en:Template:Infobox journal|Template:Infobox journal]] * [[Template:ISSN]] * [[Template:OCLC]] * [[Template:OCLC search link]] * [[Template:UPC]] {{basepage subpage| [[Category:External link templates]] [[Category:Catalog lookup templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local/ILL link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} Use {{tl|ISSN link}} to produce a link or pair of links to the [[WorldCat]] search page for an [[:en:ISSN|ISSN]]. Unlike {{tl|ISSN}}, this template does not prefix the link with "[[:en:ISSN|ISSN]] "; and if multiple ISSNs are provided they will be tagged as being for "(print)" and "(web)". ==Usage== In any context where you need to show an ISSN linked to a [[WorldCat]] search, but where the inclusion of the "[[:en:ISSN|ISSN]] " prefix would be distracting, or where you need to provide both the ISSN (print) and eISSN (web/online/digital) for a publication, you can use {{tl|ISSN link}} to generate the needed markup. The template was created for use with [[List of Cambridge University Press journals]] (several hundred ISSNs in a table, with a separate column for the ISSNs). If you only need a single or a few ISSNs, you'll be better off just using {{tl|ISSN}}. The template takes two positional parameters, and at least one is required. If both are given, the first is assumed to be the ISSN for the print edition and the second to be the online edition, and both will be tagged accordingly. If only one parameter is given, no assumption is made regarding print vs. online and no tagging is done. * |1=: If |2= is also provided, this is the ISSN for the print edition, and will be tagged with "(print)". If this is the only parameter passed in, no assumption will be made as to its type and no tag will be provided. * |2=: The ISSN of the online edition. This parameter is optional, but if given will produce a linked ISSN tagged with "(web)". ==Examples== ===One argument=== {{ISSN link|1234-5678}} → {{ISSN link|1234-5678}} ===Two arguments=== {{ISSN link|1234-5678|9876-5432}} → {{ISSN link|1234-5678|9876-5432}} ===Two arguments without a line break=== {{ISSN link|1234-5678|9876-5432|br=;}} → {{ISSN link|1234-5678|9876-5432|br=;}} ==Notes and error detection== ===Finding the ISSN of a publication=== * Find the ISSN by [http://grlinker.coalliance.org/grbin/ilu_rmt.cgi searching with the publication's name] on the [[Colorado Alliance of Research Libraries]] web site. ===Note=== An ISSN is always eight digits long. Scroll down the WorldCat page to find the ISSN. Do not rely on the digits shown in the WorldCat URL for the particular publication, since the URL sometimes shows only seven digits, which is invalid to use as the ISSN. ===Errors=== Articles with invalid ISSNs (as determined by the template) will be placed in {{cl|Articles with invalid ISSNs}}. ==See also== * [[:en:Template:Infobox newspaper|Template:Infobox newspaper]] * [[:en:Template:Infobox journal|Template:Infobox journal]] * [[Template:ISSN]] * [[Template:OCLC]] * [[Template:OCLC search link]] * [[Template:UPC]] {{basepage subpage| [[Category:External link templates]] [[Category:Catalog lookup templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local/ILL link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} Use {{tl|ISSN link}} to produce a link or pair of links to the [[WorldCat]] search page for an [[:en:ISSN|ISSN]]. Unlike {{tl|ISSN}}, this template does not prefix the link with "[[:en:ISSN|ISSN]] "; and if multiple ISSNs are provided they will be tagged as being for "(print)" and "(web)". ==Usage== In any context where you need to show an ISSN linked to a [[WorldCat]] search, but where the inclusion of the "[[:en:ISSN|ISSN]] " prefix would be distracting, or where you need to provide both the ISSN (print) and eISSN (web/online/digital) for a publication, you can use {{tl|ISSN link}} to generate the needed markup. The template was created for use with [[List of Cambridge University Press journals]] (several hundred ISSNs in a table, with a separate column for the ISSNs). If you only need a single or a few ISSNs, you'll be better off just using {{tl|ISSN}}. The template takes two positional parameters, and at least one is required. If both are given, the first is assumed to be the ISSN for the print edition and the second to be the online edition, and both will be tagged accordingly. If only one parameter is given, no assumption is made regarding print vs. online and no tagging is done. * |1=: If |2= is also provided, this is the ISSN for the print edition, and will be tagged with "(print)". If this is the only parameter passed in, no assumption will be made as to its type and no tag will be provided. * |2=: The ISSN of the online edition. This parameter is optional, but if given will produce a linked ISSN tagged with "(web)". ==Examples== ===One argument=== {{ISSN link|1234-5678}} → {{ISSN link|1234-5678}} ===Two arguments=== {{ISSN link|1234-5678|9876-5432}} → {{ISSN link|1234-5678|9876-5432}} ===Two arguments without a line break=== {{ISSN link|1234-5678|9876-5432|br=;}} → {{ISSN link|1234-5678|9876-5432|br=;}} ==Notes and error detection== ===Finding the ISSN of a publication=== * Find the ISSN by [http://grlinker.coalliance.org/grbin/ilu_rmt.cgi searching with the publication's name] on the [[Colorado Alliance of Research Libraries]] web site. ===Note=== An ISSN is always eight digits long. Scroll down the WorldCat page to find the ISSN. Do not rely on the digits shown in the WorldCat URL for the particular publication, since the URL sometimes shows only seven digits, which is invalid to use as the ISSN. ===Errors=== Articles with invalid ISSNs (as determined by the template) will be placed in {{cl|Articles with invalid ISSNs}}. ==See also== * [[:en:Template:Infobox newspaper|Template:Infobox newspaper]] * [[:en:Template:Infobox journal|Template:Infobox journal]] * [[Template:ISSN]] * [[Template:OCLC]] * [[Template:OCLC search link]] * [[:en:Template:UPC|Template:UPC]] {{basepage subpage| [[Category:External link templates]] [[Category:Catalog lookup templates]] }}

revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} Use {{tl|ISSN link}} to produce a link or pair of links to the [[WorldCat]] search page for an [[:en:ISSN|ISSN]]. Unlike {{tl|ISSN}}, this template does not prefix the link with "[[:en:ISSN|ISSN]] "; and if multiple ISSNs are provided they will be tagged as being for "(print)" and "(web)". ==Usage== In any context where you need to show an ISSN linked to a [[WorldCat]] search, but where the inclusion of the "[[:en:ISSN|ISSN]] " prefix would be distracting, or where you need to provide both the ISSN (print) and eISSN (web/online/digital) for a publication, you can use {{tl|ISSN link}} to generate the needed markup. The template was created for use with [[List of Cambridge University Press journals]] (several hundred ISSNs in a table, with a separate column for the ISSNs). If you only need a single or a few ISSNs, you'll be better off just using {{tl|ISSN}}. The template takes two positional parameters, and at least one is required. If both are given, the first is assumed to be the ISSN for the print edition and the second to be the online edition, and both will be tagged accordingly. If only one parameter is given, no assumption is made regarding print vs. online and no tagging is done. * |1=: If |2= is also provided, this is the ISSN for the print edition, and will be tagged with "(print)". If this is the only parameter passed in, no assumption will be made as to its type and no tag will be provided. * |2=: The ISSN of the online edition. This parameter is optional, but if given will produce a linked ISSN tagged with "(web)". ==Examples== ===One argument=== {{ISSN link|1234-5678}} → {{ISSN link|1234-5678}} ===Two arguments=== {{ISSN link|1234-5678|9876-5432}} → {{ISSN link|1234-5678|9876-5432}} ===Two arguments without a line break=== {{ISSN link|1234-5678|9876-5432|br=;}} → {{ISSN link|1234-5678|9876-5432|br=;}} ==Notes and error detection== ===Finding the ISSN of a publication=== * Find the ISSN by [http://grlinker.coalliance.org/grbin/ilu_rmt.cgi searching with the publication's name] on the [[:en:Colorado Alliance of Research Libraries|Colorado Alliance of Research Libraries]] web site. ===Note=== An ISSN is always eight digits long. Scroll down the WorldCat page to find the ISSN. Do not rely on the digits shown in the WorldCat URL for the particular publication, since the URL sometimes shows only seven digits, which is invalid to use as the ISSN. ===Errors=== Articles with invalid ISSNs (as determined by the template) will be placed in {{cl|Articles with invalid ISSNs}}. ==See also== * [[:en:Template:Infobox newspaper|Template:Infobox newspaper]] * [[:en:Template:Infobox journal|Template:Infobox journal]] * [[Template:ISSN]] * [[Template:OCLC]] * [[Template:OCLC search link]] * [[:en:Template:UPC|Template:UPC]] {{basepage subpage| [[Category:External link templates]] [[Category:Catalog lookup templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} Use {{tl|ISSN link}} to produce a link or pair of links to the [[:en:WorldCat|WorldCat]] search page for an [[:en:ISSN|ISSN]]. Unlike {{tl|ISSN}}, this template does not prefix the link with "[[:en:ISSN|ISSN]] "; and if multiple ISSNs are provided they will be tagged as being for "(print)" and "(web)". ==Usage== In any context where you need to show an ISSN linked to a [[:en:WorldCat|WorldCat]] search, but where the inclusion of the "[[:en:ISSN|ISSN]] " prefix would be distracting, or where you need to provide both the ISSN (print) and eISSN (web/online/digital) for a publication, you can use {{tl|ISSN link}} to generate the needed markup. The template was created for use with [[List of Cambridge University Press journals]] (several hundred ISSNs in a table, with a separate column for the ISSNs). If you only need a single or a few ISSNs, you'll be better off just using {{tl|ISSN}}. The template takes two positional parameters, and at least one is required. If both are given, the first is assumed to be the ISSN for the print edition and the second to be the online edition, and both will be tagged accordingly. If only one parameter is given, no assumption is made regarding print vs. online and no tagging is done. * |1=: If |2= is also provided, this is the ISSN for the print edition, and will be tagged with "(print)". If this is the only parameter passed in, no assumption will be made as to its type and no tag will be provided. * |2=: The ISSN of the online edition. This parameter is optional, but if given will produce a linked ISSN tagged with "(web)". ==Examples== ===One argument=== {{ISSN link|1234-5678}} → {{ISSN link|1234-5678}} ===Two arguments=== {{ISSN link|1234-5678|9876-5432}} → {{ISSN link|1234-5678|9876-5432}} ===Two arguments without a line break=== {{ISSN link|1234-5678|9876-5432|br=;}} → {{ISSN link|1234-5678|9876-5432|br=;}} ==Notes and error detection== ===Finding the ISSN of a publication=== * Find the ISSN by [http://grlinker.coalliance.org/grbin/ilu_rmt.cgi searching with the publication's name] on the [[:en:Colorado Alliance of Research Libraries|Colorado Alliance of Research Libraries]] web site. ===Note=== An ISSN is always eight digits long. Scroll down the WorldCat page to find the ISSN. Do not rely on the digits shown in the WorldCat URL for the particular publication, since the URL sometimes shows only seven digits, which is invalid to use as the ISSN. ===Errors=== Articles with invalid ISSNs (as determined by the template) will be placed in {{cl|Articles with invalid ISSNs}}. ==See also== * [[:en:Template:Infobox newspaper|Template:Infobox newspaper]] * [[:en:Template:Infobox journal|Template:Infobox journal]] * [[Template:ISSN]] * [[Template:OCLC]] * [[Template:OCLC search link]] * [[:en:Template:UPC|Template:UPC]] {{basepage subpage| [[Category:External link templates]] [[Category:Catalog lookup templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} Use {{tl|ISSN link}} to produce a link or pair of links to the [[:en:WorldCat|WorldCat]] search page for an [[:en:ISSN|ISSN]]. Unlike {{tl|ISSN}}, this template does not prefix the link with "[[:en:ISSN|ISSN]] "; and if multiple ISSNs are provided they will be tagged as being for "(print)" and "(web)". ==Usage== In any context where you need to show an ISSN linked to a [[:en:WorldCat|WorldCat]] search, but where the inclusion of the "[[:en:ISSN|ISSN]] " prefix would be distracting, or where you need to provide both the ISSN (print) and eISSN (web/online/digital) for a publication, you can use {{tl|ISSN link}} to generate the needed markup. The template was created for use with [[List of Cambridge University Press journals]] (several hundred ISSNs in a table, with a separate column for the ISSNs). If you only need a single or a few ISSNs, you'll be better off just using {{tl|ISSN}}. The template takes two positional parameters, and at least one is required. If both are given, the first is assumed to be the ISSN for the print edition and the second to be the online edition, and both will be tagged accordingly. If only one parameter is given, no assumption is made regarding print vs. online and no tagging is done. * |1=: If |2= is also provided, this is the ISSN for the print edition, and will be tagged with "(print)". If this is the only parameter passed in, no assumption will be made as to its type and no tag will be provided. * |2=: The ISSN of the online edition. This parameter is optional, but if given will produce a linked ISSN tagged with "(web)". ==Examples== ===One argument=== {{ISSN link|1234-5678}} → {{ISSN link|1234-5678}} ===Two arguments=== {{ISSN link|1234-5678|9876-5432}} → {{ISSN link|1234-5678|9876-5432}} ===Two arguments without a line break=== {{ISSN link|1234-5678|9876-5432|br=;}} → {{ISSN link|1234-5678|9876-5432|br=;}} ==Notes and error detection== ===Finding the ISSN of a publication=== * Find the ISSN by [http://grlinker.coalliance.org/grbin/ilu_rmt.cgi searching with the publication's name] on the [[:en:Colorado Alliance of Research Libraries|Colorado Alliance of Research Libraries]] web site. ===Note=== An ISSN is always eight digits long. Scroll down the WorldCat page to find the ISSN. Do not rely on the digits shown in the WorldCat URL for the particular publication, since the URL sometimes shows only seven digits, which is invalid to use as the ISSN. ===Errors=== Articles with invalid ISSNs (as determined by the template) will be placed in {{cl|Articles with invalid ISSNs}}. ==See also== * [[:en:Template:Infobox newspaper|Template:Infobox newspaper]] * [[:en:Template:Infobox journal|Template:Infobox journal]] * [[Template:ISSN]] * [[Template:OCLC]] * [[Template:OCLC search link]] * [[:en:Template:UPC|Template:UPC]] {{basepage subpage| [[Category:External link templates]] [[Category:Catalog lookup templates]] }} [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} Use {{tl|ISSN link}} to produce a link or pair of links to the [[:en:WorldCat|WorldCat]] search page for an [[:en:ISSN|ISSN]]. Unlike {{tl|ISSN}}, this template does not prefix the link with "[[:en:ISSN|ISSN]] "; and if multiple ISSNs are provided they will be tagged as being for "(print)" and "(web)". ==Usage== In any context where you need to show an ISSN linked to a [[:en:WorldCat|WorldCat]] search, but where the inclusion of the "[[:en:ISSN|ISSN]] " prefix would be distracting, or where you need to provide both the ISSN (print) and eISSN (web/online/digital) for a publication, you can use {{tl|ISSN link}} to generate the needed markup. The template was created for use with [[List of Cambridge University Press journals]] (several hundred ISSNs in a table, with a separate column for the ISSNs). If you only need a single or a few ISSNs, you'll be better off just using {{tl|ISSN}}. The template takes two positional parameters, and at least one is required. If both are given, the first is assumed to be the ISSN for the print edition and the second to be the online edition, and both will be tagged accordingly. If only one parameter is given, no assumption is made regarding print vs. online and no tagging is done. * |1=: If |2= is also provided, this is the ISSN for the print edition, and will be tagged with "(print)". If this is the only parameter passed in, no assumption will be made as to its type and no tag will be provided. * |2=: The ISSN of the online edition. This parameter is optional, but if given will produce a linked ISSN tagged with "(web)". ==Examples== ===One argument=== {{ISSN link|1234-5678}} → {{ISSN link|1234-5678}} ===Two arguments=== {{ISSN link|1234-5678|9876-5432}} → {{ISSN link|1234-5678|9876-5432}} ===Two arguments without a line break=== {{ISSN link|1234-5678|9876-5432|br=;}} → {{ISSN link|1234-5678|9876-5432|br=;}} ==Notes and error detection== ===Finding the ISSN of a publication=== * Find the ISSN by [http://grlinker.coalliance.org/grbin/ilu_rmt.cgi searching with the publication's name] on the [[:en:Colorado Alliance of Research Libraries|Colorado Alliance of Research Libraries]] web site. ===Note=== An ISSN is always eight digits long. Scroll down the WorldCat page to find the ISSN. Do not rely on the digits shown in the WorldCat URL for the particular publication, since the URL sometimes shows only seven digits, which is invalid to use as the ISSN. ===Errors=== Articles with invalid ISSNs (as determined by the template) will be placed in {{cl|Articles with invalid ISSNs}}. ==See also== * [[:en:Template:Infobox newspaper|Template:Infobox newspaper]] * [[:en:Template:Infobox journal|Template:Infobox journal]] * [[Template:ISSN]] * [[Template:OCLC]] * [[Template:OCLC search link]] * [[:en:Template:UPC|Template:UPC]] {{basepage subpage| [[Category:External link templates]] [[Category:Catalog lookup templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} Use {{tl|ISSN link}} to produce a link or pair of links to the [[:en:WorldCat|WorldCat]] search page for an [[:en:ISSN|ISSN]]. Unlike {{tl|ISSN}}, this template does not prefix the link with "[[:en:ISSN|ISSN]] "; and if multiple ISSNs are provided they will be tagged as being for "(print)" and "(web)". ==Usage== In any context where you need to show an ISSN linked to a [[:en:WorldCat|WorldCat]] search, but where the inclusion of the "[[:en:ISSN|ISSN]] " prefix would be distracting, or where you need to provide both the ISSN (print) and eISSN (web/online/digital) for a publication, you can use {{tl|ISSN link}} to generate the needed markup. The template was created for use with [[List of Cambridge University Press journals]] (several hundred ISSNs in a table, with a separate column for the ISSNs). If you only need a single or a few ISSNs, you'll be better off just using {{tl|ISSN}}. The template takes two positional parameters, and at least one is required. If both are given, the first is assumed to be the ISSN for the print edition and the second to be the online edition, and both will be tagged accordingly. If only one parameter is given, no assumption is made regarding print vs. online and no tagging is done. * |1=: If |2= is also provided, this is the ISSN for the print edition, and will be tagged with "(print)". If this is the only parameter passed in, no assumption will be made as to its type and no tag will be provided. * |2=: The ISSN of the online edition. This parameter is optional, but if given will produce a linked ISSN tagged with "(web)". ==Examples== ===One argument=== {{ISSN link|1234-5678}} → {{ISSN link|1234-5678}} ===Two arguments=== {{ISSN link|1234-5678|9876-5432}} → {{ISSN link|1234-5678|9876-5432}} ===Two arguments without a line break=== {{ISSN link|1234-5678|9876-5432|br=;}} → {{ISSN link|1234-5678|9876-5432|br=;}} ==Notes and error detection== ===Finding the ISSN of a publication=== * Find the ISSN by [http://grlinker.coalliance.org/grbin/ilu_rmt.cgi searching with the publication's name] on the [[:en:Colorado Alliance of Research Libraries|Colorado Alliance of Research Libraries]] web site. ===Note=== An ISSN is always eight digits long. Scroll down the WorldCat page to find the ISSN. Do not rely on the digits shown in the WorldCat URL for the particular publication, since the URL sometimes shows only seven digits, which is invalid to use as the ISSN. ===Errors=== Articles with invalid ISSNs (as determined by the template) will be placed in {{cl|Articles with invalid ISSNs}}. ==See also== * [[:en:Template:Infobox newspaper|Template:Infobox newspaper]] * [[:en:Template:Infobox journal|Template:Infobox journal]] * [[Template:ISSN]] * [[Template:OCLC]] * [[Template:OCLC search link]] * [[:en:Template:UPC|Template:UPC]] {{basepage subpage| [[Category:External link templates]] [[Category:Catalog lookup templates]] }} [[Category:Templates]]

revision.comment: Bot: wrap 3 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23455888809|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} Use {{tl|ISSN link}} to produce a link or pair of links to the [[:en:WorldCat|WorldCat]] search page for an [[:en:ISSN|ISSN]]. Unlike {{tl|ISSN}}, this template does not prefix the link with "[[:en:ISSN|ISSN]] "; and if multiple ISSNs are provided they will be tagged as being for "(print)" and "(web)". ==Usage== In any context where you need to show an ISSN linked to a [[:en:WorldCat|WorldCat]] search, but where the inclusion of the "[[:en:ISSN|ISSN]] " prefix would be distracting, or where you need to provide both the ISSN (print) and eISSN (web/online/digital) for a publication, you can use {{tl|ISSN link}} to generate the needed markup. The template was created for use with [[List of Cambridge University Press journals]] (several hundred ISSNs in a table, with a separate column for the ISSNs). If you only need a single or a few ISSNs, you'll be better off just using {{tl|ISSN}}. The template takes two positional parameters, and at least one is required. If both are given, the first is assumed to be the ISSN for the print edition and the second to be the online edition, and both will be tagged accordingly. If only one parameter is given, no assumption is made regarding print vs. online and no tagging is done. * |1=: If |2= is also provided, this is the ISSN for the print edition, and will be tagged with "(print)". If this is the only parameter passed in, no assumption will be made as to its type and no tag will be provided. * |2=: The ISSN of the online edition. This parameter is optional, but if given will produce a linked ISSN tagged with "(web)". ==Examples== ===One argument=== {{ISSN link|1234-5678}} → {{ISSN link|1234-5678}} ===Two arguments=== {{ISSN link|1234-5678|9876-5432}} → {{ISSN link|1234-5678|9876-5432}} ===Two arguments without a line break=== {{ISSN link|1234-5678|9876-5432|br=;}} → {{ISSN link|1234-5678|9876-5432|br=;}} ==Notes and error detection== ===Finding the ISSN of a publication=== * Find the ISSN by [http://grlinker.coalliance.org/grbin/ilu_rmt.cgi searching with the publication's name] on the [[:en:Colorado Alliance of Research Libraries|Colorado Alliance of Research Libraries]] web site. ===Note=== An ISSN is always eight digits long. Scroll down the WorldCat page to find the ISSN. Do not rely on the digits shown in the WorldCat URL for the particular publication, since the URL sometimes shows only seven digits, which is invalid to use as the ISSN. ===Errors=== Articles with invalid ISSNs (as determined by the template) will be placed in {{cl|Articles with invalid ISSNs}}. ==See also== * [[:en:Template:Infobox newspaper|Template:Infobox newspaper]] * [[:en:Template:Infobox journal|Template:Infobox journal]] * [[Template:ISSN]] * [[Template:OCLC]] * [[Template:OCLC search link]] * [[:en:Template:UPC|Template:UPC]] {{basepage subpage| }} [[Category:External link templates]] [[Category:Catalog lookup templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

revision.comment: Bot: strip HTML comments [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24938264069|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} Use {{tl|ISSN link}} to produce a link or pair of links to the [[:en:WorldCat|WorldCat]] search page for an [[:en:ISSN|ISSN]]. Unlike {{tl|ISSN}}, this template does not prefix the link with "[[:en:ISSN|ISSN]] "; and if multiple ISSNs are provided they will be tagged as being for "(print)" and "(web)". ==Usage== In any context where you need to show an ISSN linked to a [[:en:WorldCat|WorldCat]] search, but where the inclusion of the "[[:en:ISSN|ISSN]] " prefix would be distracting, or where you need to provide both the ISSN (print) and eISSN (web/online/digital) for a publication, you can use {{tl|ISSN link}} to generate the needed markup. The template was created for use with [[List of Cambridge University Press journals]] (several hundred ISSNs in a table, with a separate column for the ISSNs). If you only need a single or a few ISSNs, you'll be better off just using {{tl|ISSN}}. The template takes two positional parameters, and at least one is required. If both are given, the first is assumed to be the ISSN for the print edition and the second to be the online edition, and both will be tagged accordingly. If only one parameter is given, no assumption is made regarding print vs. online and no tagging is done. * |1=: If |2= is also provided, this is the ISSN for the print edition, and will be tagged with "(print)". If this is the only parameter passed in, no assumption will be made as to its type and no tag will be provided. * |2=: The ISSN of the online edition. This parameter is optional, but if given will produce a linked ISSN tagged with "(web)". ==Examples== ===One argument=== {{ISSN link|1234-5678}} → {{ISSN link|1234-5678}} ===Two arguments=== {{ISSN link|1234-5678|9876-5432}} → {{ISSN link|1234-5678|9876-5432}} ===Two arguments without a line break=== {{ISSN link|1234-5678|9876-5432|br=;}} → {{ISSN link|1234-5678|9876-5432|br=;}} ==Notes and error detection== ===Finding the ISSN of a publication=== * Find the ISSN by [http://grlinker.coalliance.org/grbin/ilu_rmt.cgi searching with the publication's name] on the [[:en:Colorado Alliance of Research Libraries|Colorado Alliance of Research Libraries]] web site. ===Note=== An ISSN is always eight digits long. Scroll down the WorldCat page to find the ISSN. Do not rely on the digits shown in the WorldCat URL for the particular publication, since the URL sometimes shows only seven digits, which is invalid to use as the ISSN. ===Errors=== Articles with invalid ISSNs (as determined by the template) will be placed in {{cl|Articles with invalid ISSNs}}. ==See also== * [[:en:Template:Infobox newspaper|Template:Infobox newspaper]] * [[:en:Template:Infobox journal|Template:Infobox journal]] * [[Template:ISSN]] * [[Template:OCLC]] * [[Template:OCLC search link]] * [[:en:Template:UPC|Template:UPC]] {{basepage subpage| }} [[Category:External link templates]] [[Category:Catalog lookup templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]
